annotations.title: The Push to Make Semiconductors in Space Just Took a Serious Leap Forward | Scientific American
Space Forge generates plasma aboard ForgeStar-1 satellite

content.text:
Today (Wednesday, December 31, 2025) — a positive story: Cardiff-based startup Space Forge reported that its ForgeStar‑1 orbital “micro‑factory” generated plasma and ran its onboard furnace to about 1,000 °C, a milestone toward commercial in‑space semiconductor manufacturing. ([scientificamerican.com](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/the-push-to-make-semiconductors-in-space-just-took-a-serious-leap-forward/))

Why it matters: the test shows the extreme thermal conditions needed for gas‑phase crystal growth can be created and controlled in low Earth orbit, which could enable making much higher‑purity semiconductor crystals in space (the company says space‑made crystals could be orders of magnitude cleaner — up to ~4,000× in some claims) with uses in 5G, EVs, aerospace and more. ([semiconductor-today.com](https://www.semiconductor-today.com/news_items/2025/dec/spaceforge-311225.shtml))

Would you like another feel‑good story from today (health, environment, community) or a short summary of the Space Forge coverage?
